lessons.title: AI SDR Architecture: Outbound Stack and Agent Design
lessons.title_es: Arquitectura del SDR IA: stack de salida y diseño del agente
lessons.description: Design an AI sales development rep that runs prospecting and outreach without human supervision.
lessons.description_es: Diseña un representante de desarrollo de ventas con IA que ejecute prospección y alcance sin supervisión humana.
lessons.content:
What an AI SDR Actually Does

An AI SDR (Sales Development Representative) is a software agent that autonomously executes the top-of-funnel sales workflow: identify prospects, research them, personalize outreach, send messages, handle initial replies, qualify, and book meetings — then hand off to a human AE. It does not replace sales judgment; it eliminates the 3–5 hours of manual research-and-send work per rep per day.

The Four Agent Components

  1. Prospect Sourcer: Pulls leads from Apollo, Clay, or LinkedIn Sales Navigator based on ICP (Ideal Customer Profile) criteria. ICP inputs: industry, headcount range, revenue range, tech stack signals, job postings, intent data (Bombora, G2).
  2. Research Engine: Per-prospect context gathering. Scrapes company website, LinkedIn profile, recent press releases, Crunchbase funding data. Generates a structured brief: company pains, recent events (hiring, funding, product launches), relevant trigger events.
  3. Personalization Writer: Uses the research brief as context for the LLM to generate the first line of each email — the

that makes it feel human-written. The rest of the email is templated but dynamic (merge fields, conditional blocks based on company size or vertical).
  • Sequence Executor: Manages send schedule, tracks opens/replies via pixel tracking and SMTP webhooks, branches on engagement, handles bounces, routes positive replies to a human.

    • ICP Definition Is Your Foundation

    An AI SDR is only as good as its targeting. Before writing a single email, define:

    • Company signals: SIC code, headcount 50–500, series A–C funded, specific tech stack (e.g., uses Salesforce + HubSpot)
    • Persona signals: Title must include VP/Director/Head of + [Marketing, Growth, Revenue], posted on LinkedIn in last 30 days
    • Trigger signals: Hired SDR manager in last 60 days, opened new office, launched new product

    Tight ICP = higher reply rates and fewer spam complaints. Spray-and-pray targeting will tank your domain reputation within weeks.

    Tech Stack Options

    Lightweight: Clay (enrichment + personalization) + Smartlead or Instantly (email delivery) + Zapier (orchestration). Cost: ~$400/mo. Good for <500 prospects/month.

    Enterprise: Apollo.io (sourcing) + custom Python agent (research via Firecrawl + Claude API) + Outreach or Salesloft (sequencing). Cost: $1,500–5,000/mo. Good for 2,000+ prospects/month with complex branching.

lessons.title: Email Personalization at Scale with LLMs
lessons.title_es: Personalización de correos a escala con LLMs
lessons.description: Write outbound email sequences that feel hand-crafted even when generated for thousands of prospects.
lessons.description_es: Redacta secuencias de correo saliente que parezcan hechas a mano aunque se generen para miles de prospectos.
lessons.content:
The Personalization Hierarchy

Effective AI-generated outbound has three levels of personalization. Most teams stop at level 1 and wonder why reply rates are mediocre.

  • Level 1 — Merge field: First name, company name, industry. Table stakes. Reply rate lift: minimal.
  • Level 2 — Contextual: References a recent company event, specific product, or known pain for their vertical. Requires enrichment data. Reply rate lift: 40–80% vs. Level 1.
  • Level 3 — Insight-driven: Connects a specific observation about their business to your solution. Example:

Reply rate lift: 2–5x vs. Level 1.

Prompt Engineering for Outbound Copy

The personalization prompt has four parts: Role (senior sales copywriter, direct response style), Context (company brief, persona brief), Instruction (write one 2-sentence opening that references [specific signal] and connects it to [specific pain]), Constraints (no buzzwords, no

max 40 words, conversational tone).

Always include a negative constraint list in your prompt. LLMs default to sycophantic, corporate-speak openers. Explicit prohibitions (

) dramatically improve output quality.

The 4-Step Email Sequence

Email 1 (Day 0): Personalized opener + one-sentence value prop + low-friction CTA (

). Max 100 words total.

Email 2 (Day 3): Value-add — send a relevant case study, stat, or piece of content. No hard pitch. End with the same soft CTA.

Email 3 (Day 7): Social proof + specificity. Name a recognizable customer in their vertical. Make the ROI tangible.

Email 4 (Day 14): The breakup email.

Breakup emails frequently generate the highest reply rate in the sequence.

Quality Control Pipeline

Never send LLM-generated copy without a validation layer. Use a secondary prompt to score each generated email on: specificity (does it reference a real signal?), tone match (is it conversational or corporate?), CTA clarity (is there exactly one ask?). Reject and regenerate anything scoring below threshold. This adds ~0.2 seconds per email but eliminates the cringe-worthy outputs that tank domain reputation.

lessons.title: Meeting Booking Automation and Calendar Integration
lessons.title_es: Automatización de reserva de reuniones e integración de calendario
lessons.description: Close the loop from reply to booked meeting without human involvement.
lessons.description_es: Cierra el ciclo desde la respuesta hasta la reunión reservada sin intervención humana.
lessons.content:
The Reply-to-Booking Pipeline

When a prospect replies positively, three things need to happen in under 60 seconds: classify the reply (positive, negative, not now, out of office), route it (positive → booking flow, others → human review queue), and send the availability link or propose times. Every minute of delay after a positive reply reduces booking probability.

Reply Classification

Use an LLM classifier with a structured output schema. Input: raw reply text. Output: {
}. Train your classification prompt with 10–15 examples of each intent class. Edge cases:

= question with an auto-reply containing social proof + rebook CTA.

Booking Link vs. Proposed Times

Booking links (Calendly, Chili Piper, Cal.com) have a 60–70% no-show rate when sent cold. Proposed times (

) have 2x higher confirmation rates because they create micro-commitment. Use proposed times in the first booking attempt; fall back to a link in follow-up reminders.

Connect your calendar API (Google Calendar API or Microsoft Graph) to pull real-time availability slots. Filter out back-to-back slots (leave 15-min buffers), early morning/late evening, and days the AE has flagged as focus time. Present a maximum of three options — choice overload above three reduces reply rates.

Confirmation and Reminder Sequence

Immediately on booking: calendar invite with video link, agenda (what you'll cover, expected outcomes), and a pre-meeting form (3 questions: current situation, biggest challenge, decision-making process). This pre-work increases show rates by 25% and shortens discovery calls.

Reminders: 24-hour email reminder, 1-hour SMS reminder (

). Post-meeting: automated follow-up email within 5 minutes of call end with recap template pre-filled by the meeting transcription AI (Otter.ai, Fireflies, or Gong). AE reviews and sends in under 2 minutes.

Routing to Human AE

The AI SDR's job ends at a booked, confirmed meeting. The handoff package delivered to the AE: prospect research brief, full conversation thread, ICP match score, pre-meeting form responses, and LinkedIn profile. Any AE who gets this package should be able to run the discovery call with zero additional prep.

lessons.title: Performance Monitoring, Domain Health, and Scaling
lessons.title_es: Monitoreo de rendimiento, salud de dominio y escalado
lessons.description: Run a high-volume AI SDR program without burning your sending domains or triggering spam filters.
lessons.description_es: Ejecuta un programa de SDR IA de alto volumen sin quemar tus dominios de envío ni activar filtros de spam.
lessons.content:
Domain Infrastructure for Scale

Never send outbound from your primary business domain. Set up sending domain aliases (yourcompany-hq.com, tryYourCompany.com, getYourCompany.com). Each domain should have a maximum of 2 mailboxes sending 30–50 emails per day. For 300 emails/day: 3 domains × 2 mailboxes × 50 sends = 300 daily capacity with healthy sending ratios.

Each domain needs: SPF record (include your ESP), DKIM (2048-bit key, rotated every 90 days), DMARC policy (start at p=none with RUA reporting, move to p=quarantine after 30 days of clean data). Without all three, Gmail and Outlook will sandbox or block your messages.

The KPIs That Signal Trouble

  • Open rate below 30%: Subject lines need work OR you're landing in spam. Check Google Postmaster Tools and Outlook JMRP.
  • Reply rate below 3%: ICP targeting is off or personalization is generic. Pull a sample of 50 sent emails and read them as a skeptical prospect.
  • Bounce rate above 5%: List quality problem. Add email verification (ZeroBounce, NeverBounce) before every send batch.
  • Spam complaint rate above 0.1%: Immediate action required. Pause sends, audit list source, check for purchased lists.

Warm-Up Protocol for New Domains

Use a warm-up tool (Mailreach, Warmbox, or Smartlead's built-in warm-up) for the first 4–6 weeks on any new sending domain. Warm-up sends fake emails between a network of real inboxes, auto-moves them to primary, and marks as not-spam. This builds sender reputation before you touch a real prospect. Do not skip this step — cold sending on a fresh domain will result in 30–60% spam placement within the first week.

Scaling the Agent

Scaling has two levers: volume (more domains, more mailboxes) and conversion rate (better targeting, better copy). Conversion rate improvements compound; volume improvements are linear. Prioritize CR work until your reply-to-booking rate exceeds 30% before adding domain infrastructure.

At scale, implement a feedback loop: won deals → extract commonalities from the winning prospect profiles → feed back into ICP targeting → tighten the sourcing criteria. Every quarter, re-score your entire prospect database against the updated ICP. Remove prospects who score below threshold even if they've been in sequences — stale leads degrade deliverability.
